intent: In DATA_DIR there is a 10x matrix triplet for one PBMC sample — files prefixed GSM5746268_MGI0369_1_SLAB-C1-C1 (matrix.mtx.gz, barcodes.tsv.gz, features.tsv.gz). Using scanpy, do standard QC, normalization, highly-variable genes, PCA, neighbors, Leiden clustering and a UMAP, then identify marker genes per cluster and show me a UMAP and a marker dotplot.

blocks.text: Good, I can see the three files for GSM5746268. Now let me read the skill for the standard scanpy workflow to ensure I follow the best practices.

blocks.content: {"status": "ok", "name": "scrna-qc-clustering", "description": "Standard scanpy processing for ONE scRNA-seq sample.", "body": "scRNA-seq QC + first-pass clustering (scanpy). Halt and present_plan before running on an unfamiliar dataset \u2014 the QC thresholds and the clustering resolution are dataset-dependent. Steps: sc.read_10x_mtx; calculate_qc_metrics; filter cells (n_genes>=200) & genes; filter pct_counts_mt; normalize_total(target_sum=1e4)+log1p; highly_variable_genes; scale; pca; neighbors; leiden; umap; rank_genes_groups (wilcoxon) for markers. Plots: QC violin, HVG, UMAP colored by leiden, marker dotplot."}

blocks.text: Perfect. Now let me present a plan for this analysis:

input.title: Standard scanpy QC + clustering for GSM5746268
input.summary: Load PBMC 10x matrix, QC filter, normalize, find HVG, PCA, neighbors, Leiden clustering, UMAP, and marker gene identification.

steps.title: Load 10x matrix triplet and compute QC metrics
steps.description: Read the MTX/barcodes/features files with scanpy.read_10x_mtx, calculate cell QC metrics (n_genes, n_counts, pct_counts_mt), and inspect distributions.

steps.title: Filter cells and genes
steps.description: Remove cells with <200 genes, genes expressed in <3 cells, and cells with >20% mitochondrial reads (standard PBMC thresholds).

steps.title: Normalize, log-transform, and identify highly-variable genes (HVG)
steps.description: Normalize to 1e4 counts/cell, log1p, then find HVG using scanpy defaults.

steps.title: PCA, neighbors, and Leiden clustering
steps.description: Scale to unit variance, run PCA (50 components), compute 15-neighbor graph (euclidean, PCA space), run Leiden at resolution=1.0 (or adjusted based on cluster count).
expected_outputs: AnnData with pca, neighbors, leiden cluster assignments

steps.title: UMAP and marker gene identification
steps.description: Compute UMAP on neighbors, then run rank_genes_groups (Wilcoxon test) to find top markers per cluster.

steps.title: Visualize markers via dotplot
steps.description: Select top ~5 markers per cluster, plot dotplot showing expression and detection rate across clusters.

assumptions: Data is PBMC (human), so standard thresholds apply: n_genes≥200, pct_counts_mt≤20%.
Leiden resolution=1.0 is a starting point; will adjust if needed based on cluster count.
Will use Wilcoxon test for marker detection (fast, standard).
Top 5 markers per cluster for dotplot visualization.
